Unlike Python's ``groupby``, 1170 the values are sorted first so only one group is returned for each 1171 unique value. 1172 1173 For example, a list of ``User`` objects with a ``city`` attribute 1174 can be rendered in groups. @async_variant(sync_do_groupby)  # type: ignore
async def do_groupby(
    environment: "Environment",
    value: "t.Union[t.AsyncIterable[V], t.Iterable[V]]",

input.new_string:
You can also get the number of items in each group without having to materialize the list of items by passing ``with_counts=True``. In this case, ``groupby`` yields ``(grouper, list, count)`` tuples. .. sourcecode:: jinja
    {% for city, items, count in users|groupby("city", with_counts=True) %}
  • {{ city }} ({{ count }})
    • {% endfor %}
.. versionchanged:: 3.2 Added the ``with_counts`` parameter. .. versionchanged:: 3.0 Added the ``default`` parameter. .. versionchanged:: 2.6 The attribute supports dot notation for nested access. """ expr = make_attrgetter(environment, attribute, default=default) if with_counts: out = [] for key, values in groupby(sorted(value, key=expr), expr): values = list(values) out.append(_GroupTupleWithCount(key, values, len(values))) return out return [ _GroupTuple(key, list(values)) for key, values in groupby(sorted(value, key=expr), expr) ] @async_variant(sync_do_groupby) # type: ignore async def do_groupby( environment: "Environment", value: "t.Union[t.AsyncIterable[V], t.Iterable[V]]", attribute: t.Union[str, int], default: t.Optional[t.Any] = None, with_counts: bool = False, ) -> "t.List[t.Tuple[t.Any, t.List[V]]]": expr = make_attrgetter(environment, attribute, default=default) value = await auto_to_list(value) if with_counts: out = [] for key, values in groupby(sorted(value, key=expr), expr): values = list(values) out.append(_GroupTupleWithCount(key, values, len(values))) return out return [ _GroupTuple(key, list(values)) for key, values in groupby(sorted(value, key=expr), expr) ]

for key, values in groupby(sorted(value, key=expr), expr):
            values = list(values)
            out.append(_GroupTupleWithCount(key, values, len(values)))
        return out

    return [
        _GroupTuple(key, list(values))
        for key, values in groupby(sorted(value, key=expr), expr)
    ]


@async_variant(sync_do_groupby)  # type: ignore
async def do_groupby(
    environment: "Environment",
    value: "t.Union[t.AsyncIterable[V], t.Iterable[V]]",
    attribute: t.Union[str, int],
    default: t.Optional[t.Any] = None,
    with_counts: bool = False,
) -> "t.List[t.Tuple[t.Any, t.List[V]]]":
    expr = make_attrgetter(environment, attribute, default=default)
    value = await auto_to_list(value)

    if with_counts:
        out = []
        for key, values in groupby(sorted(value, key=expr), expr):
            values = list(values)
            out.append(_GroupTupleWithCount(key, values, len(values)))
        return out

    return [

content.text: Looks good. Now run tests related to filters.
All existing groupby tests pass. Let's quickly sanity test the new feature manually.

input.command:
python -c "
from jinja2 import Environment

env = Environment()
tmpl = env.from_string(
    '{% for city, items, count in users|groupby(\"city\", with_counts=True) %}{{ city }}:{{ count }};{% endfor %}'
)
users = [
    {'city': 'NY', 'name': 'a'},
    {'city': 'NY', 'name': 'b'},
    {'city': 'LA', 'name': 'c'},
]
print(tmpl.render(users=users))

tmpl2 = env.from_string(
    '{% for city, items in users|groupby(\"city\") %}{{ city }}:{{ items|length }};{% endfor %}'
)
print(tmpl2.render(users=users))
"

python -c "
import asyncio
from jinja2 import Environment

env = Environment(enable_async=True)
tmpl = env.from_string(
    '{% for city, items, count in users|groupby(\"city\", with_counts=True) %}{{ city }}:{{ count }};{% endfor %}'
)
users = [
    {'city': 'NY', 'name': 'a'},
    {'city': 'NY', 'name': 'b'},
    {'city': 'LA', 'name': 'c'},
]
print(asyncio.run(tmpl.render_async(users=users)))
"

content.text: Both sync and async variants work correctly. Let's run the full filters test suite once more and check for peer messages before finalizing.

""" expr = make_attrgetter(environment, attribute, default=default) + + if with_counts: + out = [] + for key, values in groupby(sorted(value, key=expr), expr): + values = list(values) + out.append(_GroupTupleWithCount(key, values, len(values))) + return out + return [ _GroupTuple(key, list(values)) for key, values in groupby(sorted(value, key=expr), expr) @@ -1222,11 +1258,21 @@ async def do_groupby( value: "t.Union[t.AsyncIterable[V], t.Iterable[V]]", attribute: t.Union[str, int], default: t.Optional[t.Any] = None, + with_counts: bool = False, ) -> "t.List[t.Tuple[t.Any, t.List[V]]]": expr = make_attrgetter(environment, attribute, default=default) + value = await auto_to_list(value) + + if with_counts: + out = [] + for key, values in groupby(sorted(value, key=expr), expr): + values = list(values) + out.append(_GroupTupleWithCount(key, values, len(values))) + return out + return [ - _GroupTuple(key, await auto_to_list(values)) - for key, values in groupby(sorted(await auto_to_list(value), key=expr), expr) + _GroupTuple(key, list(values)) + for key, values in groupby(sorted(value, key=expr), expr) ]

content.text:
The patch is written to `/workspace/repo/patch.txt` and all groupby tests (122 in the filters suite) pass. 

Summary: Added an optional `with_counts` parameter (default `False`) to `sync_do_groupby`/`do_groupby` in `src/jinja2/filters.py`. When `True`, it yields `_GroupTupleWithCount(grouper, list, count)` tuples instead of `_GroupTuple(grouper, list)`, computing the count during grouping. Verified manually with both sync and async templates, and broadcast a STATUS message to agent1 (who is adding a `case_sensitive` param to the same functions) noting the parameter order so they can append theirs without clobbering my change.
